events.event_description: Caleb Cushing, commissioned as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, presented credentials to Chinese authorities, establishing the first U.S. diplomatic mission to China.

evidence.quote: Caleb Cushing, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1843-05-08. Credentials presented: 1844-06-12.

events.event_description: Nelson Trusler Johnson was promoted from Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission in China from a Legation to an Embassy.

evidence.quote: Nelson Trusler Johnson, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1929-12-16. Credentials presented: 1930-02-01. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1935-09-17. Note: Commissioned to China.

events.event_description: David K. E. Bruce was appointed Chief of the U.S. Liaison Office in Beijing, establishing a liaison-level presence with the People's Republic of China while the Embassy remained at Taipei.

evidence.quote: David Kirkpatrick Este Bruce, Chief. Appointed: 1973-03-15. Credentials presented: 1973-05-14. Left post on 1974-09-25.

events.event_description: The United States terminated diplomatic relations with the Republic of China (Taiwan) on January 1, 1979, closing Embassy Taipei on February 28, 1979, ending formal representation to the ROC government.

evidence.quote: The United States established diplomatic relations with the People's Republic of China, and terminated them with the Republic of China, on January 1, 1979. Embassy Taipei closed February 28, 1979.

events.event_description: The U.S. Liaison Office in Beijing was elevated to Embassy status, establishing full ambassadorial relations with the People's Republic of China.

evidence.quote: U.S. Liaison Office Beijing became Embassy Beijing on March 1, 1979.
